subtopic: Early Diagnosis and Follow-Up of MTC
question: For a patient with a right thyroid nodule, what is the best next step if serum calcitonin concentration is greater than 20 pg/mL but less than 100 pg/mL (5.8-29.2 pmol/L)?
options: <Option A> Wait and see; perform clinical and biochemical examination every 6 months
<Option B> Total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection
<Option D> Repeated FNA for immunocytochemistry for calcitonin and measurement of calcitonin in the needle washout
explanation: In cases where serum calcitonin is greater than 20 pg/mL but less than 100 pg/mL, it should prompt further diagnostic procedures. A calcium-stimulation test can be performed to confirm if the calcitonin is secreted by MTC.
reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: "The fact that a significant advantage in prolonging the progression-free survival time has been demonstrated by both drugs, so far neither shown an increase in overall survival."
title: Patients With oid Cancer
